channel.title: Northwatch — What Changed
channel.description: Monthly structural data updates from Northwatch: a Canadian civic data resource.

item.title: Site launched — Northwatch is live
item.description: Policy timeline, Northwatch Index, six chart categories, and 44 datasets now publicly accessible.

item.title: Northwatch Index introduced — Canada scores 31/100
item.description: Composite score across five structural pillars: Economic Health, Fiscal Sustainability, Demographic Vitality, Social Cohesion, and Labour & Equality. Rated Severe.

item.title: Fertility rate holds at 1.35 — 36th consecutive year below replacement
item.description: Canada has not reached replacement-level fertility (2.1) since 1971. The second-generation immigrant convergence data confirms immigration does not structurally resolve this.

item.title: Labour force participation falls to 64.1%
item.description: Down from a peak of 67.6% in 2008. Headline unemployment remains stable — but hundreds of thousands of working-age Canadians have exited the workforce entirely and are not counted.

item.title: Bank of Canada holds overnight rate at 2.75%
item.description: The BoC paused its rate-cut cycle amid US tariff uncertainty. Household debt at 179% of income remains acutely sensitive to any upward rate movement.
